content.problemStatement:
Consider the following operations on a triple of integers. In one operation, you should:


Choose an integer ddd and an arithmetic operation ŌĆĢ either addition or multiplication.
Choose a subset of elements of the triple.
Apply the arithmetic operation to each of the chosen elements, i.e. either add ddd to each of them or multiply each of them by ddd.
For example, if we have a triple (3,5,7)(3, 5, 7)(3,5,7), we may choose to add 333 to the first and third element, and we get (6,5,10)(6, 5, 10)(6,5,10) using one operation.

You are given an initial triple (p,q,r)(p, q, r)(p,q,r) and a target triple (a,b,c)(a, b, c)(a,b,c). Find the minimum number of operations needed to transform (p,q,r)(p, q, r)(p,q,r) into (a,b,c)(a, b, c)(a,b,c).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers ppp, qqq and rrr.
The second line contains three space-separated integers aaa, bbb and ccc.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ŌĆĢ the minimum required number of operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We add 333 to the first and third element of (3,5,7)(3, 5, 7)(3,5,7) to form (6,5,10)(6, 5, 10)(6,5,10).
Example case 2: We can add 111 to each element to form (9,7,4)(9, 7, 4)(9,7,4) and then multiply the third element by 222.
